metadata.description: A non-human identity/account — an ACTOR (automation, an agent, a provider integration, an API-key holder) that DCM authenticates and authorizes (ADR-RBAC-001). actor_type `service_account`. Holds no secret material — credentials referenced (Security.CredentialRef). Backs the built-in provider's static_api_key/service accounts.

context.purpose: Models a non-human account — automation, an agent, a provider integration — as an authenticated, auditable actor.
context.plain_description: An account for something that is not a person: a pipeline, an agent, an integration holding an API key. Like a person it has a handle, an authenticator, and referenced-only credentials; unlike a person it declares an owner — the person or team accountable for it. Its stable actor id is what role assignments and audit records bind to.
use_when: You need automation to authenticate with its own identity instead of borrowing a person's.
You need every non-human credential holder to have a named accountable owner.
not_for: The API key or token itself — Security.CredentialRef, referenced not stored.
works_with: Security.CredentialRef — the account's key/token, by reference.
Identity.Group — memberships that grant it roles.

handle.description: Service-account name, unique within the realization.

authenticated_by.description: Auth Provider that authenticates it (built-in by default).

credential_ref.description: Reference to a Security.CredentialRef (API key / token) — never inline.

actor_uuid.description: The stable actor id referenced by role-assignments and DCMGroup (access_grouping) membership.

relationships.description: Member of an access DCMGroup / Identity.Group; membership drives role binding.
